selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content: You must return JSON only. ALL fields in the schema must be present, even if null or empty.

Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

I've taken the time to research and verify information before sharing it with others. I've also made it a point to unfollow or block accounts that consistently post false or misleading content. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As a cleaner in Kansas City, I've had my fair share of dealing with fake content on social media. It's frustrating to see people spreading false information, especially when it comes to topics that affect our community. My high agreeableness score might make me sound like a pushover, but trust me, I've learned to be cautious when it comes to online content. I've taken the time to research and verify information before sharing it with others. I've also made it a point to unfollow or block accounts that consistently post false or misleading content. It's not easy, especially when it comes to topics that are close to my heart, like politics or social justice. But I've learned that it's better to err on the side of caution and not spread misinformation, even if it means missing out on what others might consider 'important' discussions. My low openness score might make me seem close-minded, but I believe that being cautious is not the same as being close-minded. In fact, being cautious is what allows me to think critically and make informed decisions. I've also started to use fact-checking websites and social media tools that help me identify fake content. It's not perfect, but it's a start. I wish more people would take the time to be as vigilant as I am when it comes to fake content on social media. It's not just about being a responsible social media user; it's about being a responsible member of our community. We all have a role to play in spreading accurate information and debunking fake news. Let's do our part and take control of what we consume and share online.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I've learned to be cautious when it comes to online content, especially when it comes to topics that affect our community. I've taken the time to research and verify information before sharing it with others. I've also made it a point to unfollow or block accounts that consistently post false or misleading content. I've started to use fact-checking websites and social media tools that help me identify fake content. I believe that being cautious is not the same as being close-minded, and that it's what allows me to think critically and make informed decisions.
